message.content:
Another Claude session sent a message:
<teammate-message teammate_id="sol-review" color="blue" summary="Confirmed HEAD db68ff84e verdict">
Confirmed with `git log --oneline -1`: HEAD is `db68ff84e fix(desktop): per-owner absence authority — snapshots prove presence, never absence (KANBAN-HOST-SOURCE, Sol round 3)`. I re-read `git show db68ff84e`.

Snapshot-authority HIGH from c83 — PASS: db68ff84e correctly removes `snapshots` from both global and per-owner authority. Snapshot rows now contribute presence only. Card `hostId` schema/default, seed/promote/rebind stamping, reconcile backfill, and per-owner wiring across prune/render/deleteCompletedCard/split/Mount are coherent.

However db68ff84e introduces/leaves two NEW HIGH false-authority paths in its new implementation:

1. HIGH — optimistic query-cache rows are classified as a live host response. In db68ff84e, `C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64\apps\desktop\src\renderer\hooks\host-workspaces\useHostWorkspaces\useHostWorkspaces.ts:257-266` defines live authority as `queries[index]?.data !== undefined`. The same file at `:284-298` manually creates that data with `queryClient.setQueryData`; `useWorkspaceCreates.ts:107-124` does so before the create mutation, and `useOptimisticCollectionActions.ts:191-209,235-248` before update/rename requests. If the real list has not answered, an optimistic upsert can make the cache `[Wnew]`, grant owner authority, and falsely mark existing Wold absent. If the list hangs/fails, db68ff84e can immediately hide/not-found/exit Wold and prune its card after 10 minutes with no undo. Fix: maintain separate successful-live-response provenance set only when `workspace.list` queryFn resolves; never infer authority from mutable cached rows.

2. HIGH — absent host ID is treated as decommissioned before `v2Hosts` is ready. db68ff84e still destructures only `{ data: hosts = [] }` at `useHostWorkspaces.ts:88-96`, then its new `isAbsenceAuthoritative` returns true for `!known.has(hostId)` at `:261-275`. During cold cache hydration, `hosts=[]` while the collection is not ready is normal; remote owners are temporarily classified as removed, causing restored splits/embedded mounts to navigate or Not Found and Kanban cards to be treated as authoritatively absent. Fix: retain `hostsReady` from `useLiveQuery`; only self-heal `!known.has(hostId)` when the hosts collection is strictly ready, otherwise return false.

Overall db68ff84e verdict: snapshot-authority HIGH PASS, but commit FAIL at NEW HIGH scope due the two issues above. No other NEW BLOCKER/HIGH found.
</teammate-message>

input.old_string: **How to apply:** any fork feature needing the workspace universe must read `useHostWorkspaces()` / host-served rows, never `collections.v2Workspaces`. Diagnosis shortcut: compare `host.db` `workspaces.cloud_synced_at` (node:sqlite works on all three DBs readOnly) + kanban daily backup JSON `~/.superset/backups/kanban/<org>-<date>.json`. Related [[project_kanban_completed_column]], [[project_sidebar_v1142_regression_and_semantic_gate]].
input.new_string:
**How to apply:** any fork feature needing the workspace universe must read `useHostWorkspaces()` / host-served rows, never `collections.v2Workspaces`. Diagnosis shortcut: compare `host.db` `workspaces.cloud_synced_at` (node:sqlite works on all three DBs readOnly) + kanban daily backup JSON `~/.superset/backups/kanban/<org>-<date>.json`. Related [[project_kanban_completed_column]], [[project_sidebar_v1142_regression_and_semantic_gate]].

**Shipped fix (KANBAN-HOST-SOURCE, 5 commits f87e61b69..00eddcccb, 2026-07-22, 5-round GPT-5.6 Sol review loop).** All fork surfaces switched to `useHostWorkspaces()`. The hard part was ABSENCE semantics — Electric rows were always-present, host lists go blank per-host on outages, and kanban cards are device-local with no undo. Authority model that survived review: `isAbsenceAuthoritative(hostId)` on the provider — absence of a row is only actionable (prune after 10-min continuous-miss grace w/ self-scheduling deadline timer, split-exit, Mount not-found, frozen-record delete) when the row's OWNING host's real `workspace.list` resolved this session, tracked via `liveAnsweredHostIds` set INSIDE the queryFn (query cache is polluted by optimistic setQueryData — never derive authority from it; snapshots prove presence, never absence); owner-host-row-removed-from-org = authoritative-gone but ONLY when the v2Hosts live query `isReady` (cold-start `[]` proves nothing); kanban cards persist an owner `hostId` (nullable zod default, seed-stamped, reconcile-backfilled). Non-authoritative missing cards render from stored snapshot instead of vanishing. Also: V2WorkspaceMount must NEVER clear the create transaction itself — `useWorkspaceCreates` clears on its tracked `completed` promise (hold-until-pane-layout), and optimistic cache rows carry `source:"host"` + `worktreePath:""` before panes exist.

---
name: electric-v2workspaces-dead-for-new-rows
description: "Upstream v1.16 offline-first migration stopped writing new workspaces to the cloud v2_workspaces table — anything fork-side reading collections.v2Workspaces misses every workspace created after the upgrade (Kanban blind-spot bug, found 2026-07-22)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: f7713565-e464-48c1-a066-92a246fadb31
  modified: 2026-07-22T21:40:17.849Z
---

Upstream desktop-v1.16.x moved workspace creation host-local-only (offline-first R1/R2): `insertLocalWorkspace` writes only `~/.superset/host/<org>/host.db` `workspaces` (column `cloud_synced_at` stays NULL — NOTHING in the tree ever sets it; `pushWorkspaceCreateToCloud`/reconciler exist only in `plans/offline-first-workspace-follow-ups.md`, and integration tests assert `cloudSyncedAt` is null after create). Renderer no longer calls cloud `v2Workspace.create`. Cloud table + Electric mirror die fully in R3.

Consequence: the Electric collection `collections.v2Workspaces` (persisted mirror `~/.superset/tanstack-db.sqlite`, table per `collection_registry`) is frozen at pre-1.16 rows. Upstream surfaces migrated to host-authoritative `useHostWorkspaces()` (host `workspace.list` merged with Electric fallback; host wins). Sidebar uses that → shows new threads. Fork Kanban (`useKanbanData`) still seeded/pruned from `collections.v2Workspaces` → every post-upgrade thread got NO card (found 2026-07-22: "driver scanning profiling", "training env for internal app", "eMAR in spoke sender", "multi user for spoke app" all `cloud_synced_at: null`, absent from board).

Also affected: `useDashboardSidebarState` snooze/archive/delete projectId fallback `collections.v2Workspaces.get(workspaceId)` — fails for Electric-missing workspaces with no local-state row (e.g. auto-included mains of new projects).

**How to apply:** any fork feature needing the workspace universe must read `useHostWorkspaces()` / host-served rows, never `collections.v2Workspaces`. Diagnosis shortcut: compare `host.db` `workspaces.cloud_synced_at` (node:sqlite works on all three DBs readOnly) + kanban daily backup JSON `~/.superset/backups/kanban/<org>-<date>.json`. Related [[project_kanban_completed_column]], [[project_sidebar_v1142_regression_and_semantic_gate]].

lines: Upstream desktop-v1.16.x moved workspace creation host-local-only (offline-first R1/R2): `insertLocalWorkspace` writes only `~/.superset/host/<org>/host.db` `workspaces` (column `cloud_synced_at` stays NULL — NOTHING in the tree ever sets it; `pushWorkspaceCreateToCloud`/reconciler exist only in `plans/offline-first-workspace-follow-ups.md`, and integration tests assert `cloudSyncedAt` is null after create). Renderer no longer calls cloud `v2Workspace.create`. Cloud table + Electric mirror die fully in R3.

Also affected: `useDashboardSidebarState` snooze/archive/delete projectId fallback `collections.v2Workspaces.get(workspaceId)` — fails for Electric-missing workspaces with no local-state row (e.g. auto-included mains of new projects).

**How to apply:** any fork feature needing the workspace universe must read `useHostWorkspaces()` / host-served rows, never `collections.v2Workspaces`. Diagnosis shortcut: compare `host.db` `workspaces.cloud_synced_at` (node:sqlite works on all three DBs readOnly) + kanban daily backup JSON `~/.superset/backups/kanban/<org>-<date>.json`. Related [[project_kanban_completed_column]], [[project_sidebar_v1142_regression_and_semantic_gate]].

+**Shipped fix (KANBAN-HOST-SOURCE, 5 commits f87e61b69..00eddcccb, 2026-07-22, 5-round GPT-5.6 Sol review loop).** All fork surfaces switched to `useHostWorkspaces()`. The hard part was ABSENCE semantics — Electric rows were always-present, host lists go blank per-host on outages, and kanban cards are device-local with no undo. Authority model that survived review: `isAbsenceAuthoritative(hostId)` on the provider — absence of a row is only actionable (prune after 10-min continuous-miss grace w/ self-scheduling deadline timer, split-exit, Mount not-found, frozen-record delete) when the row's OWNING host's real `workspace.list` resolved this session, tracked via `liveAnsweredHostIds` set INSIDE the queryFn (query cache is polluted by optimistic setQueryData — never derive authority from it; snapshots prove presence, never absence); owner-host-row-removed-from-org = authoritative-gone but ONLY when the v2Hosts live query `isReady` (cold-start `[]` proves nothing); kanban cards persist an owner `hostId` (nullable zod default, seed-stamped, reconcile-backfilled). Non-authoritative missing cards render from stored snapshot instead of vanishing. Also: V2WorkspaceMount must NEVER clear the create transaction itself — `useWorkspaceCreates` clears on its tracked `completed` promise (hold-until-pane-layout), and optimistic cache rows carry `source:"host"` + `worktreePath:""` before panes exist.

content.text:
All shipped. Status:

**Answer to your original question:** upstream v1.16 stopped writing new workspaces to the cloud table — your 3 yellow-dot threads (plus "multi user for spoke app") existed only in the local host DB, and Kanban was reading the dead cloud mirror, so they never got cards.

**Fix (5 commits, pushed to main, `00eddcccb` HEAD):**
- All fork surfaces now read host-served workspace lists: Kanban seed/prune/render + actions, card details, collapse-split, `V2WorkspaceMount`, sidebar snooze/archive/delete fallbacks, Recycle-Bin destroy, `useIsLocalWorkspace`.
- GPT-5.6 Sol ran a 5-round adversarial review loop; every BLOCKER/HIGH fixed: absence of a workspace is only ever acted on (card prune, not-found, split-exit, frozen-record delete) when its *owning host* genuinely answered — provenance tracked inside the query fetch, never from the optimistic cache or stale snapshots; 10-min grace + self-scheduling prune timer; cards stay visible from their stored snapshot during host outages. Cards now persist their owning host id (backward-compatible schema default).
- `(KANBAN-HOST-SOURCE)` marker added to FEATURES.md so nightly merges can't silently revert it.

**Build:** publish run 29963086574 is building the rebuilt `desktop-v1.16.1` Release now — I'm watching it and will ask before any install. Your 4 threads will appear in the Pending column after you update.

<tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file.
String: /**
 * What ONE source's dot shows: agent permission/working (red/yellow) win;
 * else a terminal's shell-running / background-running blue; else the agent's
 * review (green); else nothing. Blue axes exist only for terminal sources.
 */
function getSourceDisplayStatus(
	state: V2NotificationState,
	workspaceId: string,
	sourceKey: V2NotificationSourceKey,
): DisplayStatus | null {
	const entry = state.sources[sourceKey];
	const agentStatus = entry?.workspaceId === workspaceId ? entry.status : null;
	if (agentStatus === "permission" || agentStatus === "working") {
		return agentStatus;
	}
	if (sourceKey.startsWith(TERMINAL_SOURCE_PREFIX)) {
		const terminalId = sourceKey.slice(TERMINAL_SOURCE_PREFIX.length);
		if (state.shellRunningTerminals[terminalId]) {
			return "shell-running";
		}
		if (state.backgroundRunningTerminals[terminalId]) {
			return "background-running";
		}
	}
	return agentStatus;
}</tool_use_error>

// this workspace). A present entry is therefore this workspace's blue dot.
+    // (AGENT-SHELL-BLUE) OSC-133 shell-running applies to PLAIN shells only:
+    // on an agent terminal the agent CLI itself is the long-running
+    // foreground command, so command-start latches for the whole session
+    // (see the AUTO-RESUME note in host-service terminal.ts) and would paint
+    // every idle agent tab blue over its green. Agent terminals take blue
+    // only from the background-tasks axis below.

content.content:
59:| Sidebar agent chips ride the fork dot pipeline | upstream's workspace-agents-row chips (agent logo + status dot under a sidebar row) originally had their own status pipe (host binding raw `lastEventType`, 4-case map that read fork events like SubagentActive/BackgroundRunning as idle) and a broken liveness filter (`terminal_sessions.status` stays `active` forever for a closed-tab pty → chips trapped permission-red for days). Chips now derive status from the shared (AY)/(DOT-AXES) per-source primitive and liveness from the open-pane layout gate — a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |

content.content:
57	| Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |
58	| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |
59	| Sidebar agent chips ride the fork dot pipeline | upstream's workspace-agents-row chips (agent logo + status dot under a sidebar row) originally had their own status pipe (host binding raw `lastEventType`, 4-case map that read fork events like SubagentActive/BackgroundRunning as idle) and a broken liveness filter (`terminal_sessions.status` stays `active` forever for a closed-tab pty → chips trapped permission-red for days). Chips now derive status from the shared (AY)/(DOT-AXES) per-source primitive and liveness from the open-pane layout gate — a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |
60	| Sidebar per-tab chips | a sidebar workspace row with 2+ open tabs replaces its inline name-line dots with one chip PER TAB — always expanded (no hover-collapse), in exact `paneLayout.tabs[]` order, labelled with the real tab title (override → pane override → per-kind default → `Tab N`; `max-w-28` truncate + full-title tooltip), agent icon from the tab's terminal-pane binding (active pane first, generic terminal glyph for plain-shell tabs), status dot as a corner badge on the icon folded from the tab's pane sources via the shared display-status primitive, flex-wrap rows. ≤1 tab keeps the upstream look: a single folded name dot, no chips. Chip click deep-links to the workspace and focuses that tab (`terminalId`/`chatSessionId`, or a validated `tabId` param for tabs with no domain-id pane, guarded on pane-layout readiness + tab existence). With the workspace-agents-row experiment off, the row falls back to a single name dot folded across ALL tabs (never zero status surfaces) | `(TAB-CHIPS)` |
61	| Nightly self-repairs ReferenceError resolutions | the nightly merge job runs the build's cannot-find-name gate itself (pure-JS `--ignore-scripts` install, linux-safe) right after conflict resolution; on failure Opus gets the exact diagnostics and repairs ONLY those (restore dropped imports/declarations), 2 attempts, re-gated each time, still-dirty aborts as before. Converts the import-dropping-resolution class (desktop-v1.15.1 useChangesTab incident) from red-night+manual-recovery to self-healing; the build's own gate stays as defense in depth | `(REFERR-REPAIR)` |
62	| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |

file.content:
| Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |
| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. cd "C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64" && bunx biome check apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/v2-notifications/store.ts FEATURES.md 2>&1 | tail -2 && git add -A && cat > tmp/commit-msg6.txt <<'EOF'
fix(desktop): agent terminals never take OSC shell-blue (AGENT-SHELL-BLUE)

The agent CLI is itself the terminal's long-running foreground command,
so OSC-133 command-start latches shellRunningTerminals for the entire
agent session (host-service terminal.ts documents this for AUTO-RESUME;
command-end only fires when the agent exits). The shared display
primitive ranks shell-blue above agent green, and TAB-CHIPS/
CHIP-DOT-UNIFY (v1.16.1) routed tab + pane-header dots through it — so
every claude terminal started since app launch showed a blue dot while
idle ("nothing running in shell").

getSourceDisplayStatus now applies shell-running blue only to terminals
with NO agent axis data (plain shells). Agent terminals keep blue solely
from the background-tasks axis; an idle agent tab shows green again.
EOF
sed -i 's/\r$//' tmp/commit-msg6.txt && git commit -F tmp/commit-msg6.txt && git push origin main && git log --oneline -1

content.text:
Blue-dot root cause found and fixed, committed on top of v1.17.0 (which the nightly published this morning — it already contains all the Kanban fixes).

**What the blue dots were:** the `claude` process is itself the terminal's long-running shell command, so the shell-integration "command running" marker latches for the entire agent session. The new per-tab chips/unified dot pipeline you got in v1.16.1 ranks shell-blue above idle-green, so every claude terminal opened since app launch showed blue while idle. Fix: shell-blue now applies only to plain shell terminals; agent tabs show green when idle (blue only for real background tasks).

Sol is doing a single-pass review of the small diff; build triggers after. Note: **skip the downloaded 1.16.1 installer** — the nightly already published v1.17.0, and the next rebuild of v1.17.0 will include this blue-dot fix too. I'll ask when it's ready.

<teammate-message teammate_id="sol-review-2" color="blue" summary="HIGH: status absence misclassifies idle agents">
1. HIGH — `agentStatus == null` is not an agent-terminal test, so the fix still paints normal idle Claude terminals shell-blue whenever their transient status entry is absent. A visible turn-end deliberately clears the terminal source (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/components/V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts:98-103`), and viewing/marking a green result seen removes the review entry (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/v2-notifications/store.ts:251-256`). OSC-133 remains latched for the whole Claude CLI, so `apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/v2-notifications/store.ts:787-807` then sees `agentStatus == null` and returns `shell-running` across tab chips, pane headers, workspace rollups, sidebar rows, and Kanban. Thus the reported bug returns immediately for the active pane after Stop, and for a hidden green pane once opened. The gate needs durable agent-terminal identity/binding, not presence of a currently active status axis. A foreign-workspace/stolen source likewise makes `agentStatus` null and can blue the explicit tab/pane selector; the workspace rollup instead skips that existing foreign key at lines 1005-1021, so that edge can also diverge across surfaces. Plain shells with no agent identity still correctly take shell-blue; no legitimate agent consumer requires OSC shell-blue because the separate background axis remains active.
</teammate-message>

// (see the AUTO-RESUME note in host-service terminal.ts) and would paint
-    // every idle agent tab blue over its green. Agent terminals take blue
-    // only from the background-tasks axis below.
+    // every idle agent tab blue over its green. The gate is the DURABLE
+    // agentTerminals registry, not the transient sources entry — mark-seen /
+    // turn-end clear that entry while the OSC latch persists. Agent
+    // terminals take blue only from the background-tasks axis below.

input.command:
cd "C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64" && git add -A && cat > tmp/commit-msg7.txt <<'EOF'
fix(desktop): durable agent-terminal registry gates shell-blue (AGENT-SHELL-BLUE, Sol review)

Sol HIGH on 25501afe4: `agentStatus == null` is not an agent-terminal
test — mark-seen and turn-end deliberately REMOVE the transient sources
entry while the OSC-133 latch persists, so the blue dot returned the
moment a green result was viewed; a foreign-workspace (stolen-pane)
entry also nulled agentStatus and could blue the tab selector.

New `agentTerminals` registry: stamped inside the applySourceAxes funnel
on ANY agent-axis write for a terminal source (including clear-to-null),
persisted with the other axes (DOT-PERSIST), cleared only when the pty
exits. getSourceDisplayStatus gates shell-running blue on it — plain
shells keep blue; agent terminals never take OSC shell-blue on any
surface (tabs, pane headers, rollups, sidebar chips, kanban).
EOF
sed -i 's/\r$//' tmp/commit-msg7.txt && git commit -F tmp/commit-msg7.txt && git push origin main && git log --oneline -1
